metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_2130
metadata.comment:
Rationale: "Figure 5. Selective increase in AChE content in the synaptic cleft after muscle training."

"Conclusion: Overall, our study supports a functional role for the G4 AChE tetramer in the neuromuscular synapse"

11/12/2017 Pim
- The authors show that a membrane attached form of AChE is responsible for the increase of ACh-esterase activity in the muscle. The term 'synaptic cleft (GO:0043083)' is selected based on the site of activity of the non-membrane bound variants and the membrane bound variant together.. Experimental description: "Electron micrographs of cross sections through EDL muscles isolated from control (A) and trained (B) rats, specifically labeled for AChE by the Nanogold-Fas conjugate."
